title: Baydo Chasdy

{{Quote|It seems as though Chasdy was born without the ability to make an honest living.|Odanni|<ref name="Wretched" />}}
'''Baydo Chasdy'''—"'''Bay'''" to his friends—was a [[Human/Legends|Human]] [[Gender/Legends|male]] who owned the [[HyperDive Cantina]] on the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Dac/Legends|Mon Calamari]] during the [[Galactic Civil War/Legends|Galactic Civil War]]. Part of the [[Fathoms]] entertainment complex, the HyperDive's unique selling point was that the entire establishment was submerged under a [[Meter/Legends|meter]] of [[Water/Legends|water]]. A self-admitted [[Gambling/Legends|gambler]], womanizer, and [[Scoundrel/Legends|con man]], Chasdy was also a member of the [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Alliance to Restore the Republic]], operating as a [[Foster Agent]] for [[Alliance Special Operations/Legends|Alliance Special Operations]]. Chasdy was a point of contact for Rebel [[Agent/Legends|agents]] who had been stranded in the field or had their covers exposed, and it was his duty to return them to the Alliance using his personal [[Starship/Legends|starship]], the modified [[YT-1300 light freighter/Legends|YT-1300 light freighter]] ''[[Chaser]]''. His frequent absences from managing the HyperDive were explained by the multitude of scams and cons that he was always running, and it offered the perfect cover for his activities.

==Biography==
{{Quote|When bragging about his HyperDive, Chasdy likes to say, 'We're the only dive-in cantina in the galaxy.'|[[Cohden K'Reye]]|<ref name="Wretched" />}}
Baydo Chasdy was a [[Human/Legends|Human]] [[Gender/Legends|male]] who resided on the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Dac/Legends|Mon Calamari]] during the [[Galactic Civil War/Legends|Galactic Civil War]]. Known as "Bay" to his friends, Chasdy was an inveterate [[Gambling/Legends|gambler]] and womanizer who spent much of his life as a self-confessed [[Scoundrel/Legends|con man]], swindler, and liar. During the Galactic Civil War, Chasdy rented space in the [[Fathoms]] entertainment complex in [[Wildwater City]]'s [[Glitter Cay tower]] from the owner, the [[Mon Calamari/Legends|Mon Calamari]] entrepreneur and [[Crime lord/Legends|crime lord]] [[Odanni]]. In the space, he established the [[HyperDive Cantina]], a rowdy bar where [[Spacer/Legends|spacers]] could meet, drink [[Geillian ale]], play [[Sabacc/Legends|sabacc]], and watch [[Sports|sporting events]]. However, the HyperDive had one unique selling point: it was submerged under a [[Meter/Legends|meter]] of [[Water/Legends|water]]. [[Wetsuit]]s and [[Swimsuit/Legends|bathing togs]] were recommended attire, with raised [[plastech]] tables stretching from the floor to above the water level to allow patrons to rest their drinks. The bar was similarly appointed, stools were bolted to the floor, and a corner of the [[Cantina/Legends|cantina]] was dedicated to games of chance and sabacc tables. [[Viewscreen|Holoscreens]] were placed high along the walls, allowing patrons to watch sporting events such as the [[Shockball/Legends|shockball]] championship.<ref name="Wretched" />

The rental [[Galactic Credit Standard/Legends|fee]] for the HyperDive was relatively cheap, and Chasdy also gave Odanni a small percentage of his profits. Odanni believed that the ability to earn an honest living was beyond Chasdy, but the gambler refuted that claim, pointing out that owning the HyperDive made him a legitimate businessman. Despite owning the cantina, Chasdy was rarely there. He left the running of the bar to his [[Kian'thar]] [[Bartender/Legends|bartender]], [[Lliegis'Nevz]]. As Chasdy knew little about managing a business, that was seen as a fortunate turn of events, as the cantina would have been bankrupted without Lliegis'Nevz's presence.<ref name="Wretched" />

Chasdy's regular excursions away from the HyperDive were a cover. People who knew him believed that his absences were attempts to swindle someone else out of their money, or run a con game, as he was well known to run a multitude of scams at the same [[Time/Legends|time]]. They generally considered him to be harmless, with little skills as a con man, but one who was lucky. Chasdy did nothing to refute their beliefs, using it as the perfect cover for one of the most dangerous jobs in the [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Alliance to Restore the Republic]]. Operating as a [[Foster Agent]] for [[Alliance Special Operations/Legends|Alliance Special Operations]], his job was to assist orphaned operatives—those [[Agent/Legends|agents]] who had been stranded or had their cover exposed—to return to the Alliance.<ref name="Wretched" /> Chasdy's dual life was perilous; Foster Agents were always in danger of being discovered by the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]], as their names were given to operatives who were in danger of capture, who could be forced to reveal the name of their contact under interrogation.<ref name="GG9">''[[Galaxy Guide 9: Fragments from the Rim]]''</ref> Rebels in need of Chasdy's help would seek him out in the HyperDive, and he would then use his [[Starship/Legends|ship]], the modified [[YT-1300 light freighter/Legends|YT-1300 light freighter]] ''[[Chaser]]'', to deliver them safely back to the Alliance.<ref name="Wretched" />

{{RPGseed}}
[[File:Shush.jpg|thumb|right|Baydo Chasdy helps out a pair of individuals]]
Sometime during the Galactic Civil War, a group of Alliance agents were being pursued by the Empire. Chasdy was their nearest point of contact, and they had to evade Imperial pursuit, Wildwater City's shady denizens, and Odanni—who believed that capturing the group would lead to a profitable situation for herself—before reaching safety. At another time, a group of spacers was hired by [[Koll]], a [[Qwohog (species)|Qwohog]] [[Medic/Legends|medic]] for the Alliance, to find his sister, [[Kei No Kay]]. Tracking her down to [[Wave Works]], a gift shop in the Fathoms complex, the spacers discovered that she was being kept as a [[Slavery/Legends|slave]] by the [[Ishi Tib/Legends|Ishi Tib]] owner of the store, [[Jund Voon]], and was forced to create wondrous pieces of [[Artist/Legends|artwork]] for him to sell. The spacers attempted to convince Chasdy and Lliegis'Nevz of the situation at Wave Works, and to attain their help in rescuing her. Voon, aware of the spacers' intent, did not want to lose his prize, and considered contracting the services of the feared [[Quarren/Legends|Quarren]], [[Nollo Kanx]], who operated as an enforcer and [[Assassin/Legends|assassin]] for Odanni, to deal with the group.<ref name="Wretched" />
{{Endgame}}

==Personality and traits==
Baydo Chasdy was a gambling, womanizing scoundrel who cheerfully admitted to lying, cheating and swindling others. Considered incompetent and unskilled—albeit lucky—by his peers, Chasdy carefully cultivated his image to distract people from his true work as an Alliance Foster Agent. He had no head for business, and happily left the running of the HyperDive to Lleigis'Nevz, although he enjoyed bragging about the place and its unique features. Although his peers doubted his skills, Chasdy was an accomplished con man and [[forger]], able to intimidate or persuade his targets as needed. He was streetwise and stealthy, but trained in [[Unarmed combat/Legends|unarmed combat]] and the use of a [[Blaster/Legends|blaster]]. His operation of the ''Chaser'' meant he could [[Pilot/Legends|fly]] [[Realspace/Legends|space]] [[Transport/Legends|transports]], including [[Astrogation/Legends|navigating]] through [[Hyperspace/Legends|hyperspace]], as well as operating the ship's weapons, [[Deflector shield/Legends|deflector shields]], and [[Sensor/Legends|sensors]].<ref name="Wretched" />

Chasdy had knowledge of several [[Alien/Legends|alien]] [[Language/Legends|languages]], and was able to determine the value of items for sale. His running of the submerged HyperDive cantina gave him above-average swimming skills, and he was technically adept with security systems. He generally carried falsified papers and fake [[Identichip/Legends|IDs]], a [[Datapad/Legends|datapad]], several changes of [[Clothing/Legends|clothes]], a [[Chronometer/Legends|chronometer]], and a [[recording rod]], and was armed with a [[Hold-out blaster/Legends|hold-out blaster]] and a [[Blaster pistol/Legends|blaster pistol]].<ref name="Wretched" />

==Behind the scenes==
Baydo Chasdy appeared in ''[[Wretched Hives of Scum & Villainy]]'', a supplement for ''[[Star Wars: The Roleplaying Game]]'' published by [[West End Games]] in [[1997]]. The supplement was written by [[Paul Danner]], with illustrations of Chasdy provided by [[Joel Carroll]].
